transcript: [00:00-00:04] I'm a New Zealand and today I'm trying traditional Māori home cooking. [00:04-00:09] With my friend Kiwi Rugby legend Dion Murers, we started off the day in his backyard catching [00:09-00:11] the ingredients we're going to cook with. [00:11-00:13] This is my first time fishing in New Zealand. [00:13-00:15] I think we caught nine fish and I had the fun as time. [00:15-00:20] We then went back to his house till unearthed the hangi, a medley of meat-in-vegetable slow [00:20-00:22] cooked under the earth for hours. [00:22-00:26] We started to prepare the catch of the day, including this massive whole crayfish that [00:26-00:27] Dion dove for. [00:27-00:31] That muscular flesh dipped in that crayfish headbutter, it was rich, creamy and succulent. [00:31-00:35] Dion shared that the food he's preparing for us is food he grew up eating with family. [00:35-00:39] And this homemade seafood chowder, made with fresh mussels and smoked fish, is my top [00:39-00:40] bite of my New Zealand trip. [00:40-00:45] We ate it with freshly fried bread and I love how fresh, smoky and creamy that chowder [00:45-00:46] is. [00:46-00:48] But it's time to devour the snapper we just caught. [00:48-00:51] Caught literally an hour ago, this was one of the freshest fish I've ever had. [00:51-00:52] But it's time for the main event. [00:52-00:54] A board filled with hangi meat. [00:54-00:58] I started off with a lick of lamb, but then Dion started to carve this juicy pork belly [00:58-01:00] so I had to give it a try. [01:00-01:03] That fatty, rich pork belly got me laughing with joy. [01:03-01:06] Last but not least, Dion made a steam pudding for us from scratch. [01:06-01:10] Eating Dion's home cooking, we learned a lot about his culture, but also felt the [01:10-01:11] care he put into each dish.

caption: My friend @Chopstick Travel first time in Toronto, and he asked me to bring him on my Toronto Food Tour ⬇️ 1) @Momo Ghar Toronto with owner chef Sonam, he comes from a fine-dining culinary background, you can see the finesse and technique he puts into his dishes yet his dishes are also welcoming and comforting  2) @Vietnoms.ca🇨🇦🇻🇳 - @Tony H opened this spot with his mom to celebrate and share his mom’s cooking. Love this spot! caption: Only eating at Palestinian Restaurants for a full day with @Munchie Peachy 1) @FlamingStove Palestinian Style Shawarma 2) Louf Palestinian Food (invited) 3) @Kunafas  #palestinianfood #palestinian #kunafa #shawarma #eating
transcript: [00:00-00:04] Today I'm only eating a Palestinian restaurant, starting with Palestinian-style shawarma, [00:04-00:06] hidden away in the back of this hasty market. [00:06-00:09] But I'm curious, what is Palestinian-style shawarma? [00:09-00:11] The owner told me it all starts with the bread. [00:11-00:16] It's called saj bread, and it's a very thin bread that they make fresh daily, completely from scratch. [00:16-00:19] They put the bread on this dough, and it starts bubbling and cooking within seconds. [00:19-00:21] But the chicken shawarma meat is also very special. [00:21-00:25] It's marinated in Palestinian spices and yogurt for three days, [00:25-00:27] and the key move for me is this ambasauce. [00:27-00:31] It's a tangy, mango-y, vibrant sauce that goes into Palestinian-style shawarma. [00:31-00:36] But these shawarma are so big that even after cutting in a half, it looks like you get two full shawarma. [00:36-00:38] But I've never had a shawarma that tastes like this. [00:38-00:40] The chicken meat is so well seasoned from the spices. [00:40-00:43] The ambasauce gives it an incredible, vibrant kick. [00:43-00:45] This might be my favorite shawarma of all time. [00:45-00:47] For dinner, we went to this fine dining Palestinian restaurant. [00:47-00:50] You walk up, and it looks like someone's home that you're entering. [00:50-00:53] And this was one of the most meaningful dining experiences for us in 2025. [00:53-00:55] Everything in this restaurant speaks of the Palestinian people, [00:55-00:57] their identity and culture. [00:57-00:59] The plates are handcrafted in Palestine. [00:59-01:00] The wine comes from Upper Galilee. [01:00-01:02] It was such a special experience for me to try this wine. [01:02-01:05] Many of the ingredients also come from Palestine, like the olives, [01:05-01:07] and the olive oil used in many of the dishes. [01:07-01:10] My favorite dish was this lamb tenderloin bests-thirma. [01:10-01:13] The lamb is so tender, and the sauce was so frequent, so aromatic. [01:13-01:16] But for me, the most special dish here is their canoffas. [01:16-01:20] It's a canoffa that the owner and chef grew up eating in his hometown of Bethlehem. [01:20-01:22] It's so different from any canoffa I've ever had. [01:22-01:24] And that's why for dessert, I went back to my favorite canoffa shawarma. [01:24-01:26] To try another style of Palestinian canoffa, [01:26-01:29] they make your canoffas fresh every 30 minutes. [01:29-01:32] And they use a very special cheese that is imported from Palestine. [01:32-01:35] It's cheesy, surrepes, so satisfying, especially fresh. [01:35-01:36] Still my favorite canoffa of all time.

caption: Who makes the best ramen in Toronto?  Brought a bunch of folks on my first food tour to one of my favorite ramen restaurants @Tondou Ramen Toronto  #ramen #noodles #toronto #japanesefood #foodies #eating
transcript: [00:00-00:04] The number one question I get asked is who makes the best ramen in Toronto? [00:04-00:08] So today I'm bringing a bunch of friends to one of my all-time favorite ramen spots. [00:08-00:14] It's called Tondo Ramen. The owner is originally from Okinawa, so they make a very special type of Okinawa ramen. [00:14-00:18] We start off with a bunch of appetizers, and I told everyone they must order the fried chicken. [00:18-00:23] It has this tangy, creamy, unini sauce, so juicy, and so much umami. [00:23-00:27] This is probably my favorite Japanese fried chicken, so they have a bunch of different ramen you can choose from, [00:27-00:31] but there's only one ramen I ever order here. It's technically a soba. [00:31-00:36] It's their Okinawa soba. I get this every time I'm here. The noodles are very special. [00:36-00:40] They almost have this hand-pulled texture to them, giving them an incredibly satisfying chew. [00:40-00:45] They add a ton of bonito flakes to their pork broth, giving the broth an incredibly smokey aroma. [00:45-00:51] I love their thick cuts of pork belly, and in an Okinawa soba you always get fish cakes, which are super fun to eat. [00:51-00:55] This is one of my all-time favorite noodles, and I think everyone else liked it too.
address: 596 College St, Toronto, ON M6G 1B4

caption: What’s your go-to Chinese takeout order?  As an Asian Canadian, Cantonese food has been an important way for me to learn and connect with my family and Asian identity. Check out ARC World on July 12 in Toronto! Visit asianrootscollective.org/arcworld to get tickets and to see the full lineup of activities and celeb appearances. caption: My Chinatown Food Crawl (Toronto Edition) ⬇️ Thanks @Early Pete for doing this with me!  1) Hong Kong Bistro Cafe 2) Yummy Yummy Dumpling House 3) Cheng Du Street Food 4) Dim Sum King #chinatown #foodcrawl #foodtour #chinesefood #toronto #Foodie #eating

[00:17-00:20] What I love about this place is the food is consistently delicious. [00:20-00:22] But this dumpling spot on the side of the street [00:22-00:25] is another spot I've been coming to for over a decade. [00:25-00:26] It's run by the kindness owner [00:26-00:29] and we started off with her massive egg and chive dumplings. [00:29-00:30] Oh, this is so good. [00:30-00:33] This is just as good as my mother-in-law's. [00:33-00:35] We then got the chicken and green pepper dumplings. [00:35-00:37] This was my first time trying this type of dumpling. [00:42-00:45] So I wanted to see just how good Toronto's Chandu food is. [00:45-00:48] We got this massive pot of beef and chilies. [00:48-00:50] It reminded me of the hot pot I had in Chandu China. caption: We had the whole vineyard to ourselves in NZ 🤯 Thank you @Greystone Wines and @New Zealand for organizing this special dining experience for us. We were blown away by the beautiful views, delicious food made with locally sourced ingredients, the best white wines we’ve ever had, and your kindness and hospitality @ramenwifeyy  #newzealand #vineyard #travelforfood #Foodie #eating
transcript: [00:00-00:03] My wife and I are in New Zealand celebrating our 10-year anniversary. [00:03-00:07] I wanted to do something special, so today we're having a private dining experience [00:07-00:11] at one of the top vineyards in New Zealand. The place was completely empty. [00:11-00:13] It was just my wife and I enjoying a beautiful meal. [00:13-00:16] Herod was one of the best white wines we've ever had on the beautiful vineyard. [00:16-00:21] What made this meal so special was that most of the ingredients are made right on the vineyard. [00:21-00:25] For example, the hamhawk terine is made from pigs that are grown right on the vineyard. [00:25-00:28] The hamhawk kind of tasted like a pate and it had a very nice smoky taste. [00:28-00:32] It doesn't get more farm to table than this, but this was my favorite dish. [00:32-00:35] Fresh loco varihou fish with a rich bourbon sauce. [00:35-00:40] I love how this is a classic French dish, but made with loco fresh New Zealand ingredients. [00:40-00:44] The fish and cream sauce literally melts in your mouth, and you can tell my wife liked it as well. [00:44-00:48] But my wife's favorite part was the dessert. I think it was a chocolate ganache with a mango [00:48-00:53] lime sorbet, but we also had a second dessert. This was our first time eating caneles. [00:53-00:55] It was so buttery in the middle, it got me dancing.

caption: What dish reminds you of your childhood? ⬇️ 1) @Chef Dev brings us to Windies Restaurant & Sports Bar for Guyanese Chinese, food that was always present at every family gathering 2) for myself we went to @greatfountain to try one of my grandpa’s favourite Cantonese dishes, which is also my favourite Cantonese dish. [00:35-00:36] Chicken Fried Rice. [00:36-00:38] I loved the Waukei, but it was really spicy too. [00:38-00:40] Next up, I share my favorite childhood meal. [00:52-00:53] Freaking fire. [00:53-00:54] Oh, really? [00:54-00:54] Do you taste the Waukei? [00:54-00:56] Do I taste the Waukei holy crap? [00:56-00:57] This was exceptional. [01:08-01:11] Then Ashyam shared not one, but three dishes from his childhood. [01:11-01:12] The first is his mom's favorite dish. [01:12-01:13] It's called Idli. [01:13-01:16] It's the steam rice cakes that you can dip in different sauces. [01:16-01:17] The flavors were so vibrant. [01:17-01:19] And as a child, Ashyam really liked pizza. [01:19-01:21] So we ordered this pizza Dosa. [01:21-01:23] It was filled with spices, herbs, and cheese. [01:23-01:25] It was so fresh, so comforting. [01:25-01:27] And Ashyam's Dad would always order this. [01:27-01:27] It's called Uttbom. [01:27-01:29] And it's a fresh savoury pancake, [01:29-01:30] top with onions, chilies, herbs. [01:30-01:33] I learned that this Indian Doferter called Vada [01:33-01:35] inspired a Guineas Doferter called Bara. [01:35-01:37] And that's what I loved about this nostalgia food tour. caption: Replying to @Harj Dhaliwal819   #biomom #birthmom #childhood #childhoodtrauma #trauma #healingjourney
transcript: My biological mother ended up leaving me in a parking lot when I was four, but let's reel it back so I can tell you the full story. Basically when I was in her form, my mom suffered with depression, she was not happy with her life. She also really wanted me to be a boy. She is two boys now and she's really connected with them, but she was really upset that I was a girl. So when I was four, she decided that she wanted to leave. So one day she decided to buy plain tickets to her family in India and she dropped me in the parking lot of my dad's restaurant. She didn't even walk me in. She just dropped me in the parking lot and said go in, tell your dad I'm going to India and then she left. My dad's cook saw me standing outside and he decided to bring me in and my dad just assumed that she missed a walk to me in and he's and I was trying to tell him I'm like, dad, mommy said that she's going to India, she's going to India and he's like pleading to marry me because I'm four and I don't know what I'm talking about. And so after work, he decided to take me home and when we got home, that's when my dad saw. Her clothes were gone, bags were packed, like things were all gone. There was emergency cash she had, that was gone, her passport was gone. So she knew that she had left. And at that time, he was filled with anger. I remember he took one of those landlines, ripped it out of the wall and smacked it against the wall next to us. And at that point, he decided that he had to go back to his ex-wife because we're Indian and he is worried about how people would see him as a single dad with a child. So he took me to my grandmother's and said, can you take her? And my grandmother took me in. And then my dad went back to my, back to his ex-wife's wife who he had a child with and he stayed with her. And basically, once my grandma was, once I was with my grandma, I was seeing my mom here and there. She ended up coming back to Canada. And it was nice. It was actually the time that we bonded the most because she would try to make a day of fun for us. So she would take me to like the park or to like an amusement park, things like that. And I actually feel like I was bonding with her. So when I was seven, my grandma ended up dying. She got hit by a car. And after that, I had to go live with my stepmom who did not like me. She hated my mom and she did not want me there. And so basically, I ended up messaging my mom on Facebook. And because I wanted to make sure she was okay, I really missed her. And my stepmom saw that. And that was the day when she got extremely angry at me, hated me and ignored me for the rest of my life. She wanted the worst for me. And that's when it started. After that, me and my mom were just connected and talked like every year or so through the phone.
address: 100 Marmora St, North York, ON M9M 2X5, Canada

And then I kind of just said, oh, never mind. Like I kind of made something up. Like I don't know how to read pregnancy has something like that. And I'm not. And so I had realized that my dad has a lot of influence on me. So my dad is able to tell me like what I need to do. And I knew that by listening to him, I would probably do something I would really regret. Because already from him talking to me at that moment, I was looking into like abortion clinics and such. Even though I knew that's not what I wanted to do. So basically what I had decided is that if I wanted to have a mentally healthy pregnancy and the easiest way was for me to go to a shelter and have my pregnancy there. And so that's what I did because I decided that my dad was definitely not going to let me stay and there was just no possible way that this was going to work. So I went to the shelter and shortly after that I got my own place. Probably like my third trimester. I had my son. I still didn't tell my dad. And then when I was three months postpartum, I decided that. I probably tell my dad. It was a bit like this. I was taking this a bit too far. So I decided that one day I called him up when I told him that I have a son. And he told me he had suspicions because he saw me at like 30 weeks. And I told him I was just fat. And so he was not too shocked. But once he found out that I was okay, I was set up. I was still going to university. Nothing had changed in my life. He was a bit more supportive. And then he became like a really good grandpa. Like my dad really loves my son and my second son. Like he is so attached to them. So that's what happened with my dad. My mom and I were just never really close. She was never really around when I was growing up. So I spoiled her when my son was like a few months old. And she was upset with the fact that my son's dad is not Indian. So she didn't really care that I had a child very young. She was just upset that he wasn't Indian. And that was it. Her and I have never been close. So she didn't really have much of a say. But she was very disappointed and kept reiterating that. I should have been with the Indian guy. And that's it.
